name: Babi Kecap i (Pork Cooked in Soya Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 kg (1 1/4 lb) fillet of pork - or leg of pork
1 slice Of root ginger, about 2 cm (1/4")
Pepper or a pinch of chilli powder
2 tbsp Medium dry sherry or rice wine (optional)
recipeInstructions:
This dish, as its name suggests, is pork cooked in soya sauce. It is a particular favorite with my own husband and children, who always know when it is about to appear on the table because the ginger and garlic frying in the sauce smells deliciously savory.
1. Cut the pork into small cubes.
2. In a bowl, mix flour with clear soya sauce and powdered ginger until well combined.
3. Coat the pork pieces thoroughly with the mixture and let them stand for at least 30 minutes.
4. Clean and slice the mushrooms. Peel garlic and ginger, slicing them very thin (or cutting into tiny sticks).
5. Heat oil or fat in a wok or thick frying pan. Fry the meat in batches, turning occasionally, until lightly browned (about 5 minutes per batch).
6. Remove most of the oil, leaving about two tablespoons. Return to heat and fry garlic and ginger slices for one minute.
7. Add mushrooms, stirring continuously for another minute.
8. Add soya sauce, boiled water, and meat back into the pan. Mix well, season with pepper or chilli powder, and stir until well combined (about 1-2 minutes).
9. Just before serving, add sherry or rice wine and lemon juice if desired.
10. Serve hot.
This dish freezes well and is best made in larger quantities using half a leg of pork, which is more economical than buying fillet pork. If freezing Babi Kecap, do not include sherry or lemon juice during cooking. To serve from the freezer, thaw completely and heat quickly on high flame for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently. Add sherry or rice wine and lemon juice just before serving.
Makes 4 servings.
